questions.q: A particle is moving in a plane with its position coordinates given by x = 5t^2 m and y = 2t m, where t is in seconds.
questions.answer: The velocity of the particle is given by v = dx/dt i + dy/dt j. Find its velocity as a function of time.

questions.q: A body moves in a plane with its initial velocity u = 10 m/s at an angle of 60° with the x-axis. If the body experiences a uniform acceleration of 2 m/s² along the positive y-direction, find its velocity after 5 seconds.
questions.answer: Use the equations of motion in a plane to find the velocity components.

questions.q: A particle of mass 2 kg moves in a plane with its position vector given by r = 3 i - 2 j m. Find its displacement and velocity vectors.
questions.answer: The displacement vector is given by the position vector itself, and the velocity vector is given by the derivative of the position vector with respect to time.

questions.q: A projectile is fired at an angle of 45° to the horizontal with an initial velocity of 20 m/s. If air resistance is neglected, find the maximum height reached by the projectile.
questions.answer: Use the equations of motion in a plane to find the maximum height reached.

questions.q: A body moves in a plane with its position vector given by r = t i + 2t^2 j m. Find its acceleration vector.
questions.answer: The acceleration vector is given by the second derivative of the position vector with respect to time.

questions.q: A particle moves in a plane with its initial velocity u = 5 m/s at an angle of 30° with the x-axis. If it experiences a uniform acceleration of 1 m/s² along the positive x-direction, find its velocity after 6 seconds.
questions.answer: Use the equations of motion in a plane to find the velocity components.

questions.q: A body moves in a plane with its velocity given by v = (10 cos 3t) i + (10 sin 3t) j m/s. What is the magnitude of its velocity after 2 seconds?
questions.answer: The magnitude of the velocity is given by the square root of the sum of the squares of the velocity components.

questions.q: A particle of mass 1 kg moves in a plane with its position vector given by r = 2 sin ωt i + 2 cos ωt j m. Find its kinetic energy.
questions.answer: Use the equations of motion in a plane to find the velocity components and then find the kinetic energy.

questions.q: A body moves in a plane with its position vector given by r = (3t - 2) i + (2t^2 + 1) j m. Find its acceleration vector.
questions.answer: The acceleration vector is given by the second derivative of the position vector with respect to time.

questions.q: A projectile is fired at an angle of 60° to the horizontal with an initial velocity of 15 m/s. If air resistance is neglected, find the time of flight.
questions.answer: Use the equations of motion in a plane to find the time of flight.
